Those who do not trust God continually upset themselves. The sorrow of the world leads to death. The Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron, and in doing so, they reproached the Lord. They looked back with causeless discontent. See the madness of uncontrolled passions, which makes people reckless with what nature considers most precious: life itself. They would rather die as criminals under God's justice than live as conquerors in his favor. At last, they decided that instead of going forward to Canaan, they would return to Egypt. Those who do not follow God’s guidance seek their own ruin. Could they expect God’s cloud to lead them or his manna to sustain them? Even if the difficulties of conquering Canaan were as great as they imagined, the difficulties of returning to Egypt would be much greater. We complain about our situation and want to change it, but is there any place or condition in this world that does not have something to make us uneasy if we are inclined to be so? The way to improve our circumstances is to put our spirits in a better frame. See the folly of turning away from God’s ways. But people rush toward the certain and disastrous consequences of a sinful course.

Moses and Aaron were astonished to see a people reject their own blessings. Caleb and Joshua assured the people of the goodness of the land. They dismissed the difficulties in the way of obtaining it as unimportant. If people were convinced of the value of religion, they would not hesitate about its requirements. Although the Canaanites lived in walled cities, their protection had vanished. The other spies noticed their strength, but these spies noticed their wickedness. No people can be safe when they have provoked God to abandon them. Even though Israel lived in tents, they were fortified. As long as we have God’s presence with us, we need not fear the most powerful force against us. Sinners are ruined by their own rebellion. But those who, like Caleb and Joshua, faithfully risk themselves for God will surely be under his special protection and hidden from the rage of people, either on earth or in heaven.

Moses made a humble plea for Israel. In this, he was a type of Christ, who prayed for those who mistreated him. The pardon of a nation’s sin is turning away its punishment; and for that reason, Moses pleaded so earnestly. Moses argued that, consistent with God's character, in his abundant compassion, he could forgive them.

The Lord granted Moses’ prayer to the extent that he did not immediately destroy the congregation. But disbelief of the promise prevents receiving the benefit. Those who despise the pleasant land will be excluded from it. God’s promise should be fulfilled to their children. They wished to die in the wilderness; God made their sin their ruin, kept his word, and their bodies fell in the wilderness. They were forced to suffer under the burden of their own sin, which was too heavy for them to bear. You will know that I broke my promise because of your sin, for God never abandons people until they first abandon him; and you will see the consequences, which will lead to your ruin. But your children, now under twenty years old, who you, in your disbelief, said would be taken captive, I will bring into the land.

Here is the sudden death of the ten evil spies. They sinned by spreading a false report about the land of promise. Those greatly provoke God who misrepresent religion, stir up dislike for it in people’s minds, or give opportunity to those who seek to do so. Justly are complainers made mourners. If they had mourned for their sin when they were faithfully rebuked, the sentence would have been averted; but since they only mourned for the judgment, it did them no good. There is mourning in hell like that; but tears will not extinguish the flames or cool the tongue.

Some of the Israelites now wanted to go forward toward Canaan. But it was too late. If people would be as eager for heaven while their opportunity lasts as they will be when it is over, how much better off they would be! What has been a duty in its season can become sin if mistimed. Those who are outside of their duties are not under God’s protection and proceed at their own risk. God told them to go, and they refused; he forbade them, and they went anyway. This is how the carnal mind is hostile to God. They had doubted God's strength; now they relied on their own without him. And so the expedition failed accordingly; then the sentence began to be carried out that their bodies would fall in the wilderness. Any undertaking that begins with sin can never end well. The way to obtain peace with our friends and success against our enemies is to have God as our friend and remain in his love. Let us take warning from the fate of Israel, so we do not perish by the same example of disbelief. Let us go forward, depending on God’s mercy, power, promise, and truth; he will be with us and bring our souls to eternal rest.
